question_text: A student is plotting the magnetic field lines around a bar magnet. Where should the field lines be closest together?

options.label: A
options.text: At the centre of the magnet

options.label: B
options.text: Near the poles of the magnet

options.label: D
options.text: Equally spaced everywhere around the magnet

explanation: Magnetic field lines are closest together where the magnetic field is strongest. The magnetic field is strongest at the poles of a magnet, so the field lines should be drawn closest together in these regions.
